title: PREDICTIVE MODELING FOR DEPENDENCY CONFIGURATION IN KNOWLEDGE-AUGMENTED NEURAL NETWORKS
summary:
Data-dependent node-to-node knowledge sharing to increase the interpretability of the activation pattern of one or more nodes in a neural network, is implemented by a set of knowledge sharing links. Each link may comprise a knowledge providing node or other source P and a knowledge receiving node R. A knowledge sharing link can impose a node-specific regularization on the knowledge receiving node R to help guide the knowledge receiving node R to have an activation pattern that is more easily interpreted. The specification and training of the knowledge sharing links may be controlled by a cooperative human-AI learning supervisor system in which a human and an artificial intelligence system work cooperatively to improve the interpretability and performance of the client system.

ABSTRACT
Data-dependent node-to-node knowledge sharing to increase the interpretability of the activation pattern of one or more nodes in a neural network, is implemented by a set of knowledge sharing links. Each link may comprise a knowledge providing node or other source P and a knowledge receiving node R. A knowledge sharing link can impose a node-specific regularization on the knowledge receiving node R to help guide the knowledge receiving node R to have an activation pattern that is more easily interpreted. The specification and training of the knowledge sharing links may be controlled by a cooperative human-AI
 learning supervisor system in which a human and an artificial intelligence system work cooperatively to improve the interpretability and performance of the client system.

details:
RECITATION THE CLAIMSThe claims are not amended herein. The following listing of the claims is provided for convenience.
1
1. (Currently Amended) A computer-implemented method for improving performance of a neural network, the method comprising:

training, by a computer system, via machine learning, the neural network using a training data set to optimize a global objective for the neural network;
identifying, by the computer system, a set of candidate knowledge sharing links for the neural network, wherein each candidate knowledge sharing link comprises a dependency from a digital knowledge source to a knowledge receiving node in the neural network;
training, by the computer system, via machine learning, a plurality of network variants of the neural network, each version comprising a different configuration of the candidate knowledge sharing links;
after the training of each network variant, determining, by the computer system, a measure indicative of performance for each network variant;
fitting, by the computer system, a predictive model that estimates a relationship between one or more characteristics of the candidate knowledge sharing links and corresponding measures indicative of performance for the network variants;
selecting, by the computer system, based on outputs of the predictive model, a configuration of the candidate knowledge sharing links to retain in the neural network;
modifying, by the computer system, the neural network, to thereby generate a modified neural network, such that the modified neural network comprises the configuration of the candidate knowledge sharing links; and
training, by the computer system, via machine learning, the modified neural network using the training data set to improve performance with respect to the global objective.
Currently amended

2
2. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the predictive model is a regression model that estimates the relationship between the candidate knowledge sharing links and the corresponding measures indicative of performance.

Previously presented

3
3. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the dependency comprises a regularization term in a loss function that penalizes divergence between an activation of the knowledge receiving node and an activation of the digital knowledge source.
Previously presented

4
4. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the dependency comprises a soft constraint on a parameter of the knowledge receiving node based on an output of the digital knowledge source.
Previously presented

5
5. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the digital knowledge source and the knowledge receiving node are both nodes of the neural network.
Previously presented

6
6. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the digital knowledge source is a node of a second neural network distinct from the neural network.
Previously presented

7
7. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the digital knowledge source comprises a node in a symbolic knowledge graph that provides output values for use in training the neural network.
Previously presented

8
8. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the digital knowledge source comprises a node in an ontology that provides output values for use in training the neural network.
Previously presented

9
9. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the digital knowledge source comprises a node in a mereology that provides output values for use in training the neural network.
Previously presented

10
10. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the digital knowledge source and the knowledge receiving node are located in different layers of the neural network.
Previously presented

11
11. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the digital knowledge source and the knowledge receiving node are located in a same layer of the neural network.

Previously presented

12
12. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the characteristics of the candidate knowledge sharing links used as input to the predictive model include a binary indicator representing presence or absence of each link in a network variant.
Previously presented

13
13. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the measure indicative of performance comprises at least one of a classification accuracy, a loss value, a measure of interpretability, or a measure of generalization performance of the network variant.
Previously presented

14
14. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the configuration of the candidate knowledge sharing links is selected based on a comparison of one or more importance values output by the predictive model to a threshold.
Previously presented

15
15. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein training the plurality of network variants comprises fine-tuning a pre-trained version of the neural network using the training data set and the respective configuration of candidate knowledge sharing links.
Previously presented

16
16. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the predictive model is trained using a sparse regression technique.
Previously presented

17
17. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the candidate knowledge sharing links are identified by evaluating similarity metrics between feature vectors associated with nodes in the neural network and nodes of digital knowledge sources.
Previously presented

18
18. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 17, 
wherein the similarity metrics comprise correlation values.
Previously presented

19
19. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
further comprising:
 prior to generating the plurality of network variants, filtering the set of candidate knowledge sharing links based on one or more predefined criteria.

Previously presented

20
20. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 19, 
wherein the predefined criteria comprise a thresholded similarity score between a feature vector of the digital knowledge source and a feature vector of the knowledge receiving node.
Previously presented

21
21. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 19, 
wherein the predefined criteria comprise a prior performance indicator associated with a previously trained network configuration that included the candidate knowledge sharing link.
Previously presented

22
22. (Previously Presented) The method of claim 1, 
wherein:

the measure indicative of performance further comprises an interpretability metric; and selecting the configuration of candidate knowledge sharing links comprises balancing improvements in the global objective with preservation or enhancement of interpretability.

Previously presented

23
23. (Previously Presented) A computer system configured to improve performance of a neural network, the computer system comprising one or more processors and a memory storing instructions that, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the computer system to:

train the neural network using a training data set to optimize a global objective for the neural network;
identify a set of candidate knowledge sharing links for the neural network, wherein each candidate knowledge sharing link comprises a dependency from a digital knowledge source to a knowledge receiving node in the neural network;
train a plurality of network variants of the neural network, each network variant comprising a different configuration of the candidate knowledge sharing links;
after training each network variant, determine a measure indicative of performance for each network variant;
fit a predictive model that estimates a relationship between one or more characteristics of the candidate knowledge sharing links and corresponding measures indicative of performance for the network variants;
select, based on outputs of the predictive model, a configuration of the candidate knowledge sharing links to retain in the neural network;
modify the neural network to generate a modified neural network comprising the configuration

of the candidate knowledge sharing links; and
train the modified neural network using the training data set to improve performance with respect to the global objective.
Previously presented
